name: img-missing-alt
issue: The element has no `alt` attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the purpose of this image to users.
suggestion: Add a descriptive `alt` attribute (e.g., `alt="프로모션 배너"`) or `alt=""` if the image is purely decorative.

name: button-missing-accessible-name
wcag: 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A) / 1.1.1 Non-text Content (Level A)
issue: Icon-only button contains only an SVG with no text content and no `aria-label`. The button has no accessible name for assistive technologies.
suggestion: Add `aria-label="새로고침"` to the button, or include a visually-hidden text span inside it.

name: button-missing-accessible-name
wcag: 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A) / 1.1.1 Non-text Content (Level A)
issue: Icon-only button contains only an SVG with no text content and no `aria-label`. The button has no accessible name for assistive technologies.
suggestion: Add `aria-label="설정"` to the button, or include a visually-hidden text span inside it.

name: svg-missing-role-and-title
issue: SVG icons inside the icon-only buttons lack `role="img"` and `<title>` elements. They are not hidden from the accessibility tree with `aria-hidden="true"` either.
suggestion: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to each SVG (since the parent button should carry the accessible name), or add `role="img"` and a `<title>` to each SVG.

name: list-item-missing-key
issue: Items rendered via `.map()` do not have a `key` prop. While primarily a React concern, missing keys can cause unexpected DOM reconciliation that may disrupt assistive technology state.
suggestion: Add a unique `key` prop to each rendered element, e.g., `<div key={item} ...>`.

name: img-missing-dimensions
issue: Both images lack explicit `width` and `height` attributes, which causes layout shift (CLS) as images load. This can disorient users of assistive technologies when content moves unexpectedly.
suggestion: Add `width` and `height` attributes to both images, or use CSS `aspect-ratio` to reserve space.

name: div-click-handler-not-keyboard-accessible
wcag: 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A) / 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A)
issue: The <div> has an `onClick` handler but lacks `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and an `onKeyDown`/`onKeyUp` handler. Keyboard users cannot activate this interactive element.
suggestion: Either replace the <div> with a <button> element, or add `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and an `onKeyDown` handler that triggers on Enter/Space.

name: duplicate-id
issue: The component uses a hardcoded `id="stat-card"`. Since StatsCard is rendered three times on the dashboard, this produces three elements with the same ID, violating the HTML uniqueness constraint. Duplicate IDs can confuse assistive technologies and break `aria-labelledby`/`aria-describedby` references.
suggestion: Use a unique ID per instance, e.g., `id={testId}` or `id={`stat-card-${label}`}`, or remove the `id` attribute entirely if not needed.

name: hamburger-missing-aria-expanded
issue: The hamburger menu toggle button does not have `aria-expanded` to communicate whether the mobile menu is currently open or closed. Screen reader users have no way to know the menu state.
suggestion: Add `aria-expanded={menuOpen}` to the hamburger button element.

name: hamburger-label-not-dynamic
issue: The `aria-label` is always "메뉴 열기" (open menu) regardless of whether the menu is open or closed. When the menu is open, the label should indicate it can be closed.
suggestion: Make the label dynamic: `aria-label={menuOpen ? "메뉴 닫기" : "메뉴 열기"}`.

name: mobile-menu-missing-role
issue: The mobile dropdown menu is a plain <div> without a navigation landmark role. Assistive technologies cannot identify this as a navigation region.
suggestion: Change the element to `<nav aria-label="모바일 메뉴">` or add `role="navigation"` with an `aria-label`.

name: hamburger-missing-aria-controls
issue: The hamburger button does not specify `aria-controls` to programmatically link it to the menu it controls.
suggestion: Add an `id` to the mobile menu div (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-menu"`) and add `aria-controls="mobile-nav-menu"` to the hamburger button.

name: breadcrumbs-missing-nav-landmark
issue: Breadcrumbs are rendered in a plain <div> instead of a <nav> landmark with `aria-label="Breadcrumb"`. Screen reader users cannot identify this as a breadcrumb navigation.
suggestion: Wrap the breadcrumbs in `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">` and use an `<ol>` list to convey the hierarchical structure.

name: breadcrumbs-not-using-list-structure
issue: Breadcrumb items are rendered as inline spans/links without an ordered list (<ol>/<li>) structure. Assistive technologies cannot convey the number of items or the sequential nature of the navigation.
suggestion: Render breadcrumbs as `<ol><li>` items within a `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">` element.

name: breadcrumbs-missing-aria-current
issue: The current (last) breadcrumb segment does not have `aria-current="page"` to indicate it represents the current page.
suggestion: Add `aria-current="page"` to the last breadcrumb span element.

name: nav-link-missing-aria-current
issue: Active navigation links (where `pathname === link.href`) are styled differently but do not have `aria-current="page"`. The active state is communicated only visually through color change.
suggestion: Add `aria-current={pathname === link.href ? "page" : undefined}` to each navigation link.

name: dashboard-page-missing-main-landmark
issue: The dashboard page root element is a generic <div> rather than a <main> landmark. Screen reader users rely on landmarks to navigate page regions efficiently.
suggestion: Replace the outermost `<div>` with `<main>` to create a proper main content landmark.

name: dynamic-content-no-live-region
issue: Content is dynamically inserted after a 500ms delay but the container does not use `aria-live` or `role="status"`. Screen reader users will not be notified when new items appear.
suggestion: Add `aria-live="polite"` to the container of dynamically loaded items, or use `role="status"` to announce updates.
